title: The Heisenberg-Weyl-parity group its coherent states and a unified Wigner-Weyl function
abstract: The Heisenberg-Weyl group $HW(d)$ related to a $d$-dimensional Hilbert space $H(d)$, is enlarged into the Heisenberg-Weyl-parity group $HWP(d)$ that incorporates parity transformations. It consists of $2d^3$ elements, of which $d^3$ elements belong to the $HW(d)$ subgroup, and extra $d^3$ elements which are related through a Fourier transform with the former ones. It is shown that $HWP(d)$ is a generalised version of the dihedral group. The properties of operators that combine displacements and parity, are discussed. $HWP(d)$ is shown to be a solvable group, and commutators of its elements perform displacement and parity transformations of quantum states, along loops in the discrete phase space.$2d^2$ coherent states related to the $HWP(d)$ group are introduced, which consist of $d^2$ coherent states related to the $HW(d)$ subgroup, and extra $d^2$ coherent states which are related through a Fourier transform with the former ones. In noisy cases, expansion of an arbitrary state in terms of the $2d^2$ coherent states with Bargmann coefficients, is advantageous in comparison to expansion in terms of the $d^2$ coherent states related to $HW(d)$. One of the consequences of the $HWP(d)$ group, is a natural unification of the Wigner and Weyl functions. The properties of the unified Wigner-Weyl function are discussed.
summary: The paper proposes an enlarged group structure, the Heisenberg-Weyl-parity group, which integrates parity transformations into the standard Heisenberg-Weyl framework. This enlargement allows for a unified Wigner-Weyl function and a larger set of coherent states that are more robust against numerical or environmental noise. The work establishes a formal link between the symmetries of a dihedral group and discrete quantum phase space.
main_problem: The paper aims to extend the Heisenberg-Weyl group by incorporating parity transformations to create a larger Heisenberg-Weyl-parity group, seeking to unify the Wigner and Weyl functions.
main_result: The author introduces the Heisenberg-Weyl-parity group HWP(d), showing it is a solvable group of class 3, and demonstrates that its associated 2d^2 coherent states provide a more noise-robust expansion for quantum states than the standard d^2 set.
method: The study employs group theory (semidirect products, commutator analysis, and solvability classes), Fourier analysis, and numerical simulations in MATLAB to evaluate noise robustness.
model_or_system: The system consists of a finite-dimensional Hilbert space H(d) where d is an odd integer, utilizing a discrete phase space based on the ring of integers modulo d.
key_observables: Wigner-Weyl function, Wigner function, Weyl function, Husimi Q-function, and Bargmann coefficients.
important_parameters: Dimension d (specifically odd d), displacement parameters alpha and beta, and noise magnitude E.
main_assumptions: The dimension d is assumed to be odd to ensure the existence of 2^-1 in the ring Z(d); the fiducial vector must not be an eigenstate of the parity operator.
figures_summary: Table I provides numerical values for Bargmann coefficients, the extended Q-function, and the unified Wigner-Weyl function for a specific state.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the HWP(d) group structure, analyzes its algebraic properties (solvability and non-nilpotency), constructs associated coherent states, demonstrates the unification of Wigner and Weyl functions, and concludes with a numerical study on noise robustness.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for quantum information and open quantum systems because it provides a new, redundant framework for state representation that is more resilient to noise, and offers a unified phase-space formalism for discrete quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
